EIH.title: Nifty holds 24,000; 23,700 key support as Akshay Bhagwat bets on Power Grid, Adani Ports
EIH.summary: Nifty holds above 24,000, with key support at 23,700 and resistance at 24,300-24,500. Easing volatility fuels a positive outlook. Top stock picks include Power Grid for a breakout, Jubilant FoodWorks on dips, and Adani Ports for momentum. Realty's surge is seen as a rebound, with Oberoi Realty showing medium-term promise.

sector_news.title: Execution, order inflows to drive L&T growth; BEL rides defence push: Siddhartha Khemka
sector_news.summary: India's capital goods sector is experiencing robust growth, fueled by strong order backlogs and increasing project execution across infrastructure, power, and industrial segments. Government focus on development and manufacturing, alongside new-age tech investments, is driving demand. Companies are poised for steady revenue expansion and stable profitability, with key players like L&T and BEL showing strong prospects.

sector_news.title: Defence stocks set for mixed Q4; Nuvama bets on BEL, Solar Industries, and a smallcap pick
sector_news.summary: Defence sector companies anticipate mixed Q4FY26 results despite strong order books. Nuvama Institutional Equities highlights BEL, Data Patterns, and Solar Industries as top picks. While order inflows remain robust, growth momentum is expected to moderate. Defence stocks have seen a strong rebound in April, with further action anticipated following earnings announcements and geopolitical developments.

sector_news.title: F&O Talk: Nifty's 6% gains are strongest since February 2021. Is it pullback or trend-reversal?
sector_news.summary: Indian markets concluded the week on a strong note, continuing their upward trend. Buying interest in banking, auto, and consumer stocks fueled the gains. The Nifty50 and BSE Sensex saw significant advances. Volatility eased considerably. Experts provided outlooks for Nifty and Bank Nifty, along with sector-specific strategies for the upcoming week.

sector_news.title: IOI Properties plans Malaysia REIT with assets worth $1.9 billion
sector_news.summary: IOI Properties Group is set to launch a Malaysian real estate investment trust valued at approximately $1.9 billion. This REIT will encompass a diverse portfolio of retail, office, and hotel assets. The company plans to offer a significant portion of units, aiming to raise substantial funds for debt repayment and future investments.

sector_news.title: Papa, aise pakdo jaise aapki picture ki heroine ho.: At 84, Ranjeet goes viral for fitness video with daughter’s guidance
sector_news.summary: Veteran actor Ranjeet, 84, has gone viral with a workout video featuring a cable chest fly, garnering over 5.6 million views. His daughter, Divyanka Bedi, guided him through the exercise, adding a light-hearted touch. Celebrities and fans praised his fitness and energy, calling it one of the 'cutest things on the internet'.

sector_news.title: US stocks today: US stocks end mixed, notches weekly gains as investors parse Middle East negotiations
sector_news.summary: US stocks closed mixed as investors weighed Middle East tensions and Iran peace talks. Inflation rose due to energy shocks, while Dow and S&P slipped but Nasdaq gained on tech strength. Despite volatility, all major indexes posted weekly gains as traders stayed cautious ahead of major earnings and macro risks.

sector_news.title: The rupee’s swing: When currency becomes the deal maker or breaker
sector_news.summary: Rupee volatility has become central to India’s distressed deals, driving stress for firms with dollar debt despite stable operations. Elevated hedging costs and FX uncertainty are reshaping pricing, deal structures, and timelines, with investors favouring export-linked assets and natural hedges.
sector_news.content: Rupee volatility has become central to India’s distressed deals, driving stress for firms with dollar debt despite stable operations. Elevated hedging costs and FX uncertainty are reshaping pricing, deal structures, and timelines, with investors favouring export-linked assets and natural hedges.

sector_news.summary: Vishal Nirmiti has secured Securities and Exchange Board of India approval for its IPO, comprising a fresh issue and offer for sale, with proceeds aimed at working capital, debt reduction and supporting growth across railway and EPC infrastructure segments

sector_news.title: TCS CEO calls Street "wrong" on AI threat; posts $40-bn order book and $2.3 billion in AI revenue for FY26
sector_news.summary: TCS CEO K Krithivasan dismisses fears of IT giants faltering in an AI-first era, asserting the company is actively leading the AI transition. He argues system integrators remain crucial for integrating new tech, driving transformative projects, and redesigning business processes. Enterprise AI spending is now focused on operational efficiency, modernization, and AI transformation itself, with a bullish outlook for FY27.

sector_news.title: Tata Investment, Tata Chemicals shares surge up to 8% after Mistry backs Tata Sons listing push
sector_news.summary: Shares of select Tata group companies moved sharply higher after Shapoorji Pallonji Mistry backed the case for listing Tata Sons, calling it a "necessary revolution" rather than a regulatory compulsion. Tata Chemicals rose as much as 8%, while Tata Investment Corporation gained around 6%, reflecting renewed investor interest in holding company and group-linked plays.

sector_news.title: Mint Explainer | IBC's clean slate: How new law gives firms a true fresh start, voids old debt claims
sector_news.summary: The amended Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code ensures new investors are not liable for past dues from any creditor, including government agencies, effective from 2016. This aims to boost investments in distressed assets.
sector_news.content: The amended Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code ensures new investors are not liable for past dues from any creditor, including government agencies, effective from 2016. This aims to boost investments in distressed assets.

global_hospitality_news.title: What is gold and silver price prediction for Monday, and will precious metals continue to rise or fall again? Analysts insights, market outlook and what should investors do now
global_hospitality_news.summary: What is gold and silver price prediction for Monday, and will precious metals continue to rise or fall again? Gold moved toward a weekly gain as the US dollar weakened after a US-Iran truce. Inflation data and interest rate expectations also shaped sentiment. Silver and other metals moved higher during the week. Investors now watch geopolitical risks, interest rates, and inflation signals to understand the next move for precious metals.
